page_title: Automation & Internal Tools for Small Businesses | Robotics Consulting
page_desc: I build the small tools and integrations that get growing businesses out of WhatsApp, Excel and paper — less manual work, fewer mistakes. Based in Riga, working across Europe.
hero_title: Less busywork. Fewer things slipping through the cracks.
hero_lead: I help small and growing businesses get out of the WhatsApp-and-Excel routine — building the internal tools and automations that quietly handle the repetitive work in the background.
hero_lead_mobile: I build the tools and automations that take repetitive work off your plate.
concept_auto_sub: The same work, minus the manual steps — fewer mistakes and a lot less time lost.
kpi_1_sub: hands-on, building real tools and integrations
kpi_2_sub: a first working prototype, so you can test the idea early
kpi_3_sub: you end up with working software, not a slide deck
problem_body: Growing businesses tend to run into the same daily headaches:
problem_body_mobile: Common daily problems in growing businesses:
problem_q1: Too much manual work and the same tasks over and over
problem_q2: Everything lives in WhatsApp, Excel, or on paper
problem_q3: No real visibility into what is actually happening
problem_q4: People keep asking around because there's no clear process
problem_note: This is exactly the kind of thing I fix — usually with one small tool, not a big project.
services_sub: A few clear ways to work together, depending on where you're stuck.
services_sub_mobile: A few clear ways to work together.
services_card_1_sub: I look at how your day-to-day actually runs, find where the most time leaks, then work out what's worth fixing first.
services_card_1_sub_mobile: I find where time leaks and what to fix first.
services_card_2_sub: I build one useful thing you can start using right away — before anyone commits to a bigger project.
services_card_2_sub_mobile: One useful tool you can use right away, before a bigger project.
services_card_3_sub: I connect your tools and systems so data ends up where it needs to be — without anyone retyping it.
services_card_3_sub_mobile: Connect tools and systems so data flows without retyping.
services_li_1: Internal tools and lightweight business systems
services_li_2: Automating orders, invoicing, and repetitive flows
services_li_3: Excel / CSV imports and data validation
services_li_4: Connecting your website to other business tools
services_li_5: Simple dashboards and better operational visibility
cta_mid_title: Want to find out what is worth fixing first?
process_sub: From the first call to something that actually works.
process_sub_mobile: From first call to a working solution.
process_step_1: We talk through what you're dealing with and what a good outcome looks like.
process_step_2: I come and see how things actually run day-to-day.
process_step_3: Together we pick the change that'll make the biggest difference soonest.
process_step_4: You get a clear first step: what I'll build, by when, and what it'll do.
process_step_5: I build it and stay with your team while they get used to it.
tech_sub: I work best with growing businesses where a lot still runs on manual steps, half-remembered processes, and tools that don't talk to each other.
tech_sub_mobile: Best fit for growing businesses with manual, messy operations.
ideal_1: Small warehouses and local fulfilment operations
ideal_3: Light manufacturing, workshops, and production businesses
ideal_4: Growing service businesses with chaotic or manual processes
faq_sub: Real questions I get from small businesses taking their first steps with tools or automation.
faq_sub_mobile: Short answers to common first-step questions.
faq_q1: How much does this typically cost?
faq_a1: It depends on the job, and I like to keep it transparent. A small review or a lightweight tool can start from a few hundred euros. Bigger integrations and automation projects vary. Either way, you'll get a clear scope and price before I start anything.
faq_q2: Do we need to have systems or vendors already selected?
faq_a2: No. I can start from scratch — picking the right tools, improving what you've got, or just working out where to begin. I'm not tied to any vendor, so the advice is about what fits your situation.
faq_q3: Can you work with our existing systems and tools?
faq_a3: Yes — and usually that's the whole point. Most jobs are about connecting or improving what you already run, not ripping it out and starting over. Integrations on top of existing systems are my main thing.
faq_q4: What if it turns out nothing needs to be automated?
faq_a4: Then I'll say so. If a small process change or a spreadsheet template fixes it, that's what I'll tell you. I'm not here to build things you don't actually need.
faq_q5: What kinds of businesses do you work with?
faq_a5: Mostly small and growing businesses — warehouses, logistics, local manufacturing, workshops, and service operations — where the daily work still feels too manual or too chaotic.
cta_sub: One short call is usually enough for me to understand your situation and point you to a sensible first step.
cta_sub_mobile: One short call is enough to suggest a sensible first step.
contact_sub: Leave your details and a line about what you're dealing with — I'll reply by email to set up a short call.
contact_message_ph: e.g. We track every order in Excel and it's getting messy…
